popularStory.headline: Robert de Beaumont, 2nd Earl of Leicester (1104-5 April 1168)
popularStory.description:
Robert de Beaumont, 2nd Earl of Leicester (1104-5 April 1168) was Justiciar of England 1155-1168.

The surname "de Beaumont" is given him by genealogists. The only known contemporary surname applied t …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester於1104年11月24日出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特時，他的父親Robert Beaumont57歲，母親Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois23歲。他於1120年11月25日與Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil在法國布列塔尼結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他於1155年被列為chief justiciar of england 。他去世於1168年4月5日在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester was born on 24 November 1104, in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, his father, Robert Beaumont, was 57 and his mother, Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, was 23. He married Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil on 25 November 1120, in Brittany, France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 9 daughters. In 1155, his occupation is listed as chief justiciar of england . He died on 5 April 1168, in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester est né le 24 novembre 1104, à Leicester, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Beaumont, avait 57 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil le 25 novembre 1120, à Bretagne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 9 filles. En 1155, sa profession de son indiquée est chief justiciar of england . Il est décédé le 5 avril 1168, à Leicester, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester am 24. November 1104 in Leicester, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Beaumont 57 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois 23. Er hat am 25. November 1120 in Bretagne, Frankreich Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 9 Töchter. 1155 ist sein Beruf als chief justiciar of england  aufgeführt. Er starb am 5. April 1168 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil il 25 novembre 1120, nel Bretagne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 9 figlie. Nel 1155, la sua occupazione è indicata come chief justiciar of england . Morì il 5 aprile 1168, nel Leicester, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicesterが1104年11月24日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスターで生まれた時，父Robert Beaumontは57歳，母Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandoisは23歳であった。1120年11月25日にフランスブレタグンでAmice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuilと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。1155年，職業はchief justiciar of england と記録されている。彼は1168年4月5日にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスターで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスター アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Leicester the Castle View, Leicestershir: 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 레이스스터 테 카스틀 비w
ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester(이)가 1104년 11월 24일 영국 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Beaumont(은)는 57세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1120년 11월 25일 프랑스 브레타그니에서 Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 1155년, 그의 직업은 chief justiciar of england 이었다. 그는 1168년 4월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 레이스스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester nasceu em 24 de novembro de 1104, em Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Beaumont, tinha 57 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil em 25 de novembro de 1120, em Bretagne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 9 filhas. Em 1155, sua profissão aparece como chief justiciar of england . Ele faleceu em 5 de abril de 1168, em Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Leicester the Castle View, Leicestershir: лисестир т кастл вию, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester родился 24 ноября 1104 г. в местности лисестир, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Beaumont было 57 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil 25 ноября 1120 г. в местности бретагн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 9 дочерей. В 1155 году он работал по профессии chief justiciar of england . Он умер 5 апреля 1168 г. в местности лисестир, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности лисестир эбби, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester nació el 24 de noviembre de 1104, en Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Beaumont, tenía 57 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil el 25 de noviembre de 1120, en Bretagne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 9 hijas. En 1155, su ocupación registrada era chief justiciar of england . Murió el 5 de abril de 1168, en Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Leicester Abbey, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
